texts: Decided to rewrite the core engine in Rust for memory safety and performance.
Migrated the storage layer from RocksDB to redb for simpler compilation.
Chose Tantivy for full-text search; BM25 is a mature and reliable algorithm.
Used gRPC instead of REST for the API layer to enforce strong type contracts.
The user prefers dark mode and dislikes harsh white backgrounds.
The user is a Vim user and has been using it for over 10 years.
The user favors functional programming style and avoids excessive mutable state.
HIPPMEM is a Rust-native associative memory engine designed to give AI agents long-term memory.
The project started in 2025 with the core idea of discovering associations at write time rather than assembling them at query time.
The engine uses no external database; all storage is built on redb + Tantivy.
Discovered that HNSW index search slowed down at million-scale data; root cause was excessive distance calculations.
By adding IVF coarse quantization pre-filtering, HNSW search speed improved by 5x.
Chinese tokenization previously only supported whitespace splitting; integrated jieba to solve Chinese entity extraction.
The user starts working at 8 AM every day and spends the first 30 minutes reading tech blogs.
The user prefers doing code reviews in the afternoon, believing focus is sharper at that time.
The user does a weekly summary and next-week planning every Friday afternoon.
Uses GitHub Actions for CI/CD, automatically running tests and linting.
Monitoring uses Prometheus + Grafana; logging uses Loki.
Deployed on Kubernetes, managed with Helm charts.
